evidence_quotes.quote: In recent years, it has become more and more common for digital games distrubiters to sell their goods in such a way that the consumer does not hold any right to experience the product they have bought.
to stop companies from ‘selling’ games under this type of licence from the outset.

paragraphs.text: In recent years, it has become more and more common for digital games distrubiters to sell their goods in such a way that the consumer does not hold any right to experience the product they have bought. This has become the norm for the vast majority of large and medium-sized distrubiters and leads to the closure of the infrastructure necessary for these games to be perceived. I believe that protecting the consumer in this scenario of continuing to experience the game by some way when the company no longer knows that he can extract currency from the consumer would have been a very appropriate and important thing to add to that legislation. And to stop companies from ‘selling’ games under this type of licence from the outset. This affects millions of European citizens both from businesses inside and outside the European Union.
